a:r.a:t: Begin as widening of the shaft on medial and lateral surfaces.
Lateral epicondyle lies proximal to the
and provides origin for the anconeus and superficial head of the supinator muscles as well as the common extensor tendon*.
Medial epicondyle lies proximal to the
and provides origin for the pronator teres muscle as well as the common flexor tendon**.
*Common extensor tendon: Extensor carpi radialis brevis, extensor digitorum, extensor digiti minimi, and extensor carpi ulnaris.
**Common flexor tendon: flexor carpi radialis, palmaris longus, flexor carpi ulnaris, and flexor digitorum superficialis.
